# host_user (Resource)

Manages one local host user without managing its password.

Use this resource to bootstrap the same user configured as the provider `target_user`. The provider cannot depend on a resource from its own configuration, so a fresh machine needs a two-step bootstrap when the target user does not exist yet:

```shell
terraform apply -target=host_user.target
terraform apply
```

For that first apply, set provider `home_dir` explicitly so user-scoped paths can resolve before the account exists.

## Example Usage

```terraform
provider "host" {
  target_user = "dongho"
  home_dir    = "/Users/dongho"
}

resource "host_group" "developers" {
  name = "developers"
}

resource "host_user" "target" {
  name      = "dongho"
  full_name = "Dongho Jung"
  home_dir  = "/Users/dongho"
  shell     = "/bin/zsh"

  groups = [
    "admin",
    host_group.developers.name,
  ]

  lifecycle {
    prevent_destroy = true
  }
}
```

## Import

Import an existing user by local username:

```shell
terraform import host_user.target dongho
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Local username.

### Optional

- `create_home` (Boolean) Create the user's home directory when creating the user. On Linux, changing `home_dir` later moves the home directory when this is true.
- `full_name` (String) User full name/comment. Omit to leave this field unmanaged.
- `groups` (Set of String) Supplementary groups managed by this resource. Groups outside this set are left untouched.
- `home_dir` (String) User home directory. Omit to use the operating system default and leave this field unmanaged after creation.
- `remove_home_on_destroy` (Boolean) Remove the user's home directory when destroying the resource. Defaults to false.
- `shell` (String) User login shell. Omit to use the operating system default and leave this field unmanaged.

### Read-Only

- `gid` (String) Numeric primary group ID as reported by the operating system.
- `id` (String) Resource identifier, equal to `name`.
- `uid` (String) Numeric user ID as reported by the operating system.
